data.title: AlphaValue/Baader Europe Lifts Hikma's Price Target on 'Favorable' Long-Term Trends
data.description:
AlphaValue/Baader Europe raised Hikma Pharmaceuticals' price target by 1.99% to 25.74 pounds sterling, saying the company is strongly positioned to benefit from "favorable" long-term industry trends.

"While we have moderated our 2025-26 earnings estimates, we have increased our out-year growth assumptions for sales and EBITDA to 3% (vs. 2% previously) to factor in Hikma's better-than-expected longer-term outlook," the research firm said Wednesday. Analysts noted an upward revision potential to their forecasts, depending on the company's achievement of its 2030 objectives, including $5 billion in sales.

AlphaValue/Baader Europe added that the London-listed pharmaceutical company's medium-to-long-term targets are supported by both internal and external factors, such as its focus on complex generics or biosimilars for chronic diseases and patent expirations, among others.

The company's add rating was maintained.

data.title: Oxley Holdings Buys Back 50,000 Shares
data.description:
Oxley Holdings bought back 50,000 shares in the open market on Thursday for SG$3,495, or SG$0.069 apiece, according to a same-day filing with the Singapore Exchange.

The company has been authorized to buy back around 211.5 million shares under its current mandate, and has so far bought back 9.6 million shares.

data.title: Singapore to Review Corporate Governance Code
data.description:
The Monetary Authority of Singapore is looking to review its code on corporate governance in order to enhance best practices and disclosures among listed companies, according to a company release on Thursday.

A corporate governance advisory committee will conduct the review in an effort to make listed companies more transparent and attractive.

data.title: Palm slips on weaker Chicago soyoil but still set for weekly gain
data.description:
Malaysian palm oil futures fell on Friday, snapping a five-session rally, pressured by weaker rival Chicago soyoil, although the market remained on track for a weekly gain.

The benchmark palm oil contract for August delivery on the Bursa Malaysia Derivatives Exchange slid 72 ringgit, or 1.83%, to 3,860 ringgit ($910.38) a metric ton at the midday break.

The contract has gained 1.99% so far this week.

Crude palm oil futures traded lower tracking weakness in the Chicago soybean oil market, said David Ng, a proprietary trader at Kuala Lumpur-based trading firm Iceberg X Sdn Bhd.

"We identify support at 3,800 ringgit and resistance at 3,950 ringgit," he said.

Dalian's most-active soyoil contract (DBYcv1) fell 0.93%, while its palm oil contract lost 1.16%. Soyoil prices on the Chicago Board of Trade were down 2.91%.

Palm oil tracks price movements of rival edible oils, as it competes for a share of the global vegetable oils market.

Oil prices were on track for a second consecutive weekly decline, weighed down by expectations of another OPEC+ output hike in July and fresh uncertainty after the latest legal twist kept U.S. President Donald Trump's tariffs in place. O/R

Weaker crude oil futures make palm a less attractive option for biodiesel feedstock.

The ringgit , palm's currency of trade, remained unchanged against the U.S. dollar.

Palm oil may retrace to 3,886 ringgit per ton, as it has broken support at 3,912 ringgit, Reuters technical analyst Wang Tao said. TECH/C

($1 = 4.2400 ringgit)

data.title: South African Markets - Factors to watch on May 30
data.description:
The following scheduled economic events, company announcements, equity and currency market moves may affect South African markets on Friday.

ECONOMIC EVENTS

April money supply and credit data (ZAM3=ECI), (ZACRED=ECI)

April trade balance (ZATBAL=ECI)

April budget balance (ZABUDM=ECI)

Inflation-linked bond auction

COMPANY NEWS

Dis-Chem Pharmacies reported a 20% rise in full-year earnings.

SOUTH AFRICAN MARKETS

South Africa's rand, government bonds and stocks strengthened on Thursday after the South African Reserve Bank trimmed its main lending rate, citing well-contained inflation.

GLOBAL MARKETS

Stocks slipped in Asia on Friday and the U.S. dollar drooped with Treasury yields as investors digested an appeals court decision to keep President Donald Trump's tariffs in effect, a day after markets rallied on a ruling to block most of them.

WALL STREET

U.S. stocks ended higher on Thursday as shares of Nvidia gained after its quarterly results, while investors digested a late-afternoon court ruling that reinstated the most sweeping of President Donald Trump's tariffs.

GOLD

Gold prices fell on Friday amid a slight uptick in dollar, while investors awaited a key U.S. inflation report that may provide further insight into the Federal Reserve's policy trajectory.

EMERGING MARKETS

For the top emerging markets news, double click on

data.title: AA Plus Tradelink's CFO Resigns
data.description: Metal products manufacturer AA Plus Tradelink said its chief financial officer, Devjit Chakraborty, has resigned from the company with effect from Thursday, citing personal reasons, according to a same-day filing to the BSE.

data.title: United Overseas Bank Buys Back, Cancels Shares Worth Over SG$9 Million
data.description:
United Overseas Bank repurchased 255,000 shares in the open market on Wednesday for around SG$9.1 million, according to a same-day filing with the Singapore Exchange.

The Singapore-based lender canceled 155,000 shares while holding 100,000 shares as treasury stock.

The bank has been authorized to buy back about 83.5 million shares under its latest mandate. To date, it has bought back around 3.9 million shares.

data.title: Nippon Steel: Conversion From Blast Furnace Would Require Capital Investment, Lead to Higher Production Costs
India's Birlasoft drops on BOBCaps' downgrade to "sell"
data.description:
** Birlasoft shares drop 4.7% to 400.90 rupees

** BOBCaps downgrades tier II IT services provider to "sell" from "hold", cuts PT from 402 rupees to 342 rupees

** Brokerage's PT lowest among 11 analysts; median PT at 448 rupees - data compiled by LSEG

** BOBCaps calls BIRS "weakest Q4 performer" among tier II IT firms that include Persistent Systems and Mphasis

** Says BIRS' weak FY26 start and subdued deal wins would mean flattish revenue; flags co's high risk to discretionary spends

** Q4 revenue was below its estimates, hurt by extended furloughs and project ramp downs - BOBCaps

** BIRS, MBFL "buy"; PERS "hold" - avg ratings per data compiled by LSEG

** YTD decline - BIRS 28%, PERS 13%, MBFL 10%

data.title: Sanofi, Regeneron's Itepekimab Delivers Mixed Results in Late-Stage Trials
data.description:
By Nina Kienle

Sanofi and Regeneron said that drug candidate Itepekimab met the primary goal in one of two chronic obstructive pulmonary disease phase 3 studies, but didn't hit the main objective of a second trial.

The French pharmaceutical company on Friday said that Itepekimab in former smokers with inadequately controlled chronic obstructive pulmonary disease met the primary endpoint of a statistically significant reduction in moderate or severe acute exacerbations compared to placebo of 27% at week 52.

However, a second phase three study didn't meet primary endpoint, although a benefit was seen earlier in the trial, it said.

Itepekimab was generally well tolerated in both studies. Sanofi and Regeneron are assessing the data and will discuss with regulatory authorities to evaluate next steps, it said.

Write to Nina Kienle at nina.kienle@wsj.com

data.title: UOB-Kay Hian Purchases Additional Shares in Thai Subsidiary
data.description:
UOB-Kay Hian , through its unit UOB-Kay Hian (BVI), acquired 8,500 additional shares in its Thai subsidiary, UOB Kay Hian Securities Thailand (SET:UOBKH), at 5.4676 baht apiece.

Following the purchase, the total number of shares held directly by UOB-Kay Hian in the Thai entity remains unchanged at 70.65%, according to a Thursday filing with the Singapore Exchange.

data.title: Synopsys halts China sales due to US export restrictions, internal memo shows
data.description:
Semiconductor design software firm Synopsys has told staff in China to halt services and sales in the country and stop taking new orders to comply with new U.S. export restrictions, according to an internal letter reviewed by Reuters.

The U.S. has ordered a broad swathe of companies to stop shipping goods to China without a license and revoked licenses already granted to certain suppliers, Reuters reported on Wednesday, citing people familiar with the matter.

Products affected include design software and chemicals for semiconductors, they said.

Synopsys on Thursday suspended its annual and quarterly forecasts after it received a letter from the Bureau of Industry and Security of the U.S. Department of Commerce, informing it of new export restrictions related to China.

The internal letter sent to staff in China on Friday said "based on our initial interpretation, these new restrictions broadly prohibit the sales of our products and services in China and are effective as of May 29, 2025."

To ensure compliance, Synopsys said it was blocking sales and fulfillment in China and halting new orders until it receives further clarification.

The measures affect all customers in China, including employees of global customers working at sites in China and Chinese military users wherever they are located, the letter added.

The steps Synopsys is taking in light of the new restrictions have not been previously reported.

Synopsys did not immediately reply to a request for comment.

Alongside Cadence and Siemens EDA, Synopsys is among the top three companies that dominate electronic design automation (EDA) software that chipmakers can use to design semiconductors used in everything from smartphones to computers and cars.

Restricting Chinese firms' access to EDA tools would be a big blow to the industry as Chinese chip design customers heavily rely on top-of-the-line U.S. software.

Synopsys, Cadence and Siemens's Mentor Graphics control more than 70% of China's EDA market, Chinese state news agency Xinhua reported in April.

Chinese companies that have said they use Synopsys and Cadence software include design firm Brite Semiconductor, Zhuhai Jieli and semiconductor IP portfolio provider VeriSilicon.

The letter sent to staff in China on Friday also said that Chinese customers' access to its customer support portal SolvNetPlus had been disabled.

data.title: Beamtree Holdings Enters Strategic Partnership with NHS Confederation in the UK, Shares Rise 4%
data.description:
Beamtree Holdings entered a strategic partnership with the NHS Confederation, which represents approximately 120 NHS Trusts in England, for a five-year term with an extension option for another five years, according to a Friday ASX filing.

The partnership is expected to be launched near the end of the year, with a membership price of 50,000 pounds sterling per annum for each hospital trust. The first 500,000 pounds of membership income will be 100% remitted to Beamtree to cover the cost of establishing the platform in the UK, and thereafter will be shared equally.

Beamtree's expected investment is expected to be around AU$1.5 million over the current fiscal year and fiscal year 2026.

Its shares rose 4% in recent trading on Friday.
